# Copyright (c) 2026, Bharatbodh and contributors
# For license information, please see license.txt

import frappe
from frappe import _


def execute(filters=None):
    filters = filters or {}

    if not filters.get("project"):
        return [], []

    columns = get_columns(filters)
    data = get_data(filters)

    return columns, data


def get_columns(filters):
    """Build columns based on filter toggles"""

    # Section 1: Default columns (always visible)
    columns = [
        {"label": _("Project"), "fieldname": "project", "fieldtype": "Link", "options": "Project", "width": 120},
        {"label": _("Machine Code"), "fieldname": "machine_code", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 120},
        {"label": _("WBS Code"), "fieldname": "cost_center", "fieldtype": "Link", "options": "Cost Center", "width": 150},
        {"label": _("M-Code"), "fieldname": "m_code", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 100},
        {"label": _("G-Code"), "fieldname": "g_code", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 100},
        {"label": _("Image"), "fieldname": "component_image", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 80},
        {"label": _("Item No"), "fieldname": "item_code", "fieldtype": "Link", "options": "Item", "width": 150},
        {"label": _("Item Name"), "fieldname": "item_name", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 200},
        {"label": _("Description"), "fieldname": "description", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 200},
        {"label": _("Make/Buy"), "fieldname": "make_or_buy", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 80},
        {"label": _("Qty"), "fieldname": "total_qty_limit", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 80},
        {"label": _("UOM"), "fieldname": "stock_uom", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 70},
    ]

    # Section 4: Material Request columns
    if filters.get("show_mr"):
        columns += [
            {"label": _("MR No"), "fieldname": "mr_nos", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 150},
            {"label": _("MR Qty"), "fieldname": "mr_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 80},
            {"label": _("MR ETA"), "fieldname": "mr_eta", "fieldtype": "Date", "width": 100},
            {"label": _("Pending for MR"), "fieldname": "pending_mr_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 100},
        ]

    # Section 5: RFQ & Supplier Quotation columns
    if filters.get("show_rfq"):
        # Vendor 1-4 columns (4 vendors x 4 fields = 16 columns)
        for i in range(1, 5):
            columns += [
                {"label": _(f"Vendor {i}"), "fieldname": f"vendor_{i}_name", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 120},
                {"label": _(f"V{i} Rate"), "fieldname": f"vendor_{i}_rate", "fieldtype": "Currency", "width": 90},
                {"label": _(f"V{i} Total"), "fieldname": f"vendor_{i}_total", "fieldtype": "Currency", "width": 100},
            ]

        # Comparison columns
        columns += [
            {"label": _("Lowest Vendor"), "fieldname": "lowest_vendor", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 120},
            {"label": _("Lowest Rate"), "fieldname": "lowest_rate", "fieldtype": "Currency", "width": 90},
            {"label": _("Decided Vendor"), "fieldname": "decided_vendor", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 120},
            {"label": _("Decided Rate"), "fieldname": "decided_rate", "fieldtype": "Currency", "width": 90},
        ]

    # Section 6: Purchase Order & Delivery columns
    if filters.get("show_po"):
        columns += [
            {"label": _("PO No"), "fieldname": "po_nos", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 150},
            {"label": _("PO Date"), "fieldname": "po_date", "fieldtype": "Date", "width": 100},
            {"label": _("PO Qty"), "fieldname": "po_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 80},
            {"label": _("Pending for PO"), "fieldname": "pending_po_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 100},
            {"label": _("GRN No"), "fieldname": "grn_nos", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 150},
            {"label": _("Delivery Qty"), "fieldname": "delivery_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 100},
            {"label": _("Delivery Date"), "fieldname": "delivery_date", "fieldtype": "Date", "width": 100},
            {"label": _("Pending Delivery"), "fieldname": "pending_delivery_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 110},
            {"label": _("Delivery Status"), "fieldname": "delivery_status", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 100},
        ]

    return columns


def get_data(filters):
    """Fetch all Component Masters for the project with procurement data"""

    # Get all Component Masters for the project
    component_masters = frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT
            pcm.name as component_master,
            pcm.project,
            COALESCE(pcm.cost_center, cc.name) as cost_center,
            pcm.machine_code,
            pcm.item_code,
            pcm.item_name,
            pcm.description,
            pcm.make_or_buy,
            pcm.total_qty_limit,
            pcm.component_image,
            pcm.parent_component,
            item.stock_uom
        FROM `tabProject Component Master` pcm
        LEFT JOIN `tabItem` item ON item.name = pcm.item_code
        LEFT JOIN `tabCost Center` cc ON cc.custom_machine_code = pcm.machine_code
        WHERE pcm.project = %(project)s
        ORDER BY cost_center, pcm.item_code
    """, filters, as_dict=True)

    if not component_masters:
        return []

    # Build parent lookup for M-Code and G-Code traversal
    parent_lookup = build_parent_lookup(filters.get("project"))

    # Build procurement data maps
    procurement_data = get_procurement_data(filters.get("project"))

    data = []
    for pcm in component_masters:
        row = {
            "component_master": pcm.component_master,
            "project": pcm.project,
            "machine_code": pcm.machine_code,
            "cost_center": pcm.cost_center,
            "m_code": get_ancestor_code(pcm.component_master, "M", parent_lookup),
            "g_code": get_ancestor_code(pcm.component_master, "G", parent_lookup),
            "component_image": pcm.component_image,
            "item_code": pcm.item_code,
            "item_name": pcm.item_name,
            "description": pcm.description,
            "make_or_buy": pcm.make_or_buy,
            "total_qty_limit": pcm.total_qty_limit,
            "stock_uom": pcm.stock_uom,
        }

        # Add Material Request data
        if filters.get("show_mr"):
            mr_data = procurement_data.get("mr", {}).get(pcm.component_master, {})
            row.update({
                "mr_nos": mr_data.get("doc_names", ""),
                "mr_qty": mr_data.get("qty", 0),
                "mr_eta": mr_data.get("eta"),
                "pending_mr_qty": (pcm.total_qty_limit or 0) - (mr_data.get("qty") or 0),
            })

        # Add RFQ & Supplier Quotation data
        if filters.get("show_rfq"):
            sq_data = procurement_data.get("sq", {}).get(pcm.component_master, {})
            vendors = sq_data.get("vendors", [])

            # Populate vendor columns (up to 4 vendors)
            for i in range(1, 5):
                if i <= len(vendors):
                    v = vendors[i-1]
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_name"] = v.get("supplier")
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_rate"] = v.get("rate")
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_total"] = v.get("amount")
                else:
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_name"] = None
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_rate"] = None
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_total"] = None

            # Comparison columns
            row["lowest_vendor"] = sq_data.get("lowest_vendor")
            row["lowest_rate"] = sq_data.get("lowest_rate")
            row["decided_vendor"] = sq_data.get("decided_vendor")
            row["decided_rate"] = sq_data.get("decided_rate")

        # Add Purchase Order & Delivery data
        if filters.get("show_po"):
            po_data = procurement_data.get("po", {}).get(pcm.component_master, {})
            pr_data = procurement_data.get("pr", {}).get(pcm.component_master, {})

            po_qty = po_data.get("qty") or 0
            delivery_qty = pr_data.get("qty") or 0

            row.update({
                "po_nos": po_data.get("doc_names", ""),
                "po_date": po_data.get("date"),
                "po_qty": po_qty,
                "pending_po_qty": (pcm.total_qty_limit or 0) - po_qty,
                "grn_nos": pr_data.get("doc_names", ""),
                "delivery_qty": delivery_qty,
                "delivery_date": pr_data.get("date"),
                "pending_delivery_qty": po_qty - delivery_qty,
                "delivery_status": get_delivery_status(po_qty, delivery_qty),
            })

        data.append(row)

    return data


def build_parent_lookup(project):
    """Build a lookup dict for parent traversal: {component_master: {item_code, parent_component}}"""
    records = frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT name, item_code, parent_component
        FROM `tabProject Component Master`
        WHERE project = %s
    """, project, as_dict=True)

    return {r.name: {"item_code": r.item_code, "parent_component": r.parent_component} for r in records}


def get_ancestor_code(component_master, prefix, parent_lookup, max_depth=20):
    """
    Traverse up the parent_component chain to find an ancestor
    whose item_code starts with the given prefix (e.g., 'M' or 'G')
    """
    current = component_master
    depth = 0

    while current and depth < max_depth:
        data = parent_lookup.get(current)
        if not data:
            break

        item_code = data.get("item_code") or ""
        if item_code.startswith(prefix):
            return item_code

        current = data.get("parent_component")
        depth += 1

    return None


def get_procurement_data(project):
    """Fetch all procurement records and build lookup maps"""

    procurement_records = frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT
            cpr.parent as component_master,
            cpr.document_type,
            cpr.document_name,
            cpr.quantity,
            cpr.rate,
            cpr.amount
        FROM `tabComponent Procurement Record` cpr
        INNER JOIN `tabProject Component Master` pcm ON pcm.name = cpr.parent
        WHERE pcm.project = %s
    """, project, as_dict=True)

    # Initialize result structure
    result = {
        "mr": {},   # Material Request
        "sq": {},   # Supplier Quotation
        "po": {},   # Purchase Order
        "pr": {},   # Purchase Receipt
    }

    # Group records by component_master and document_type
    for rec in procurement_records:
        cm = rec.component_master
        doc_type = rec.document_type

        if doc_type == "Material Request":
            if cm not in result["mr"]:
                result["mr"][cm] = {"doc_names": [], "qty": 0, "eta": None}
            result["mr"][cm]["doc_names"].append(rec.document_name)
            result["mr"][cm]["qty"] += rec.quantity or 0

        elif doc_type == "Supplier Quotation":
            if cm not in result["sq"]:
                result["sq"][cm] = {"vendors": [], "lowest_vendor": None, "lowest_rate": None}
            result["sq"][cm]["vendors"].append({
                "supplier": get_supplier_from_sq(rec.document_name),
                "rate": rec.rate,
                "amount": rec.amount,
            })

        elif doc_type == "Purchase Order":
            if cm not in result["po"]:
                result["po"][cm] = {"doc_names": [], "qty": 0, "date": None}
            result["po"][cm]["doc_names"].append(rec.document_name)
            result["po"][cm]["qty"] += rec.quantity or 0
            # Get decided vendor info
            if cm not in result["sq"]:
                result["sq"][cm] = {"vendors": [], "lowest_vendor": None, "lowest_rate": None}
            result["sq"][cm]["decided_vendor"] = get_supplier_from_po(rec.document_name)
            result["sq"][cm]["decided_rate"] = rec.rate

        elif doc_type == "Purchase Receipt":
            if cm not in result["pr"]:
                result["pr"][cm] = {"doc_names": [], "qty": 0, "date": None}
            result["pr"][cm]["doc_names"].append(rec.document_name)
            result["pr"][cm]["qty"] += rec.quantity or 0

    # Post-process: join doc_names, find lowest vendor, get dates
    for cm, data in result["mr"].items():
        data["doc_names"] = ", ".join(list(set(data["doc_names"])))
        data["eta"] = get_mr_eta(data["doc_names"].split(", ")[0] if data["doc_names"] else None)

    for cm, data in result["sq"].items():
        # Sort vendors by rate and find lowest
        if data["vendors"]:
            data["vendors"].sort(key=lambda x: x.get("rate") or float("inf"))
            data["lowest_vendor"] = data["vendors"][0].get("supplier")
            data["lowest_rate"] = data["vendors"][0].get("rate")

    for cm, data in result["po"].items():
        data["doc_names"] = ", ".join(list(set(data["doc_names"])))
        data["date"] = get_po_date(data["doc_names"].split(", ")[0] if data["doc_names"] else None)

    for cm, data in result["pr"].items():
        data["doc_names"] = ", ".join(list(set(data["doc_names"])))
        data["date"] = get_pr_date(data["doc_names"].split(", ")[0] if data["doc_names"] else None)

    return result


def get_supplier_from_sq(sq_name):
    """Get supplier name from Supplier Quotation"""
    if not sq_name:
        return None
    return frappe.db.get_value("Supplier Quotation", sq_name, "supplier")


def get_supplier_from_po(po_name):
    """Get supplier name from Purchase Order"""
    if not po_name:
        return None
    return frappe.db.get_value("Purchase Order", po_name, "supplier")


def get_mr_eta(mr_name):
    """Get schedule_date from Material Request Item"""
    if not mr_name:
        return None
    return frappe.db.get_value("Material Request", mr_name, "schedule_date")


def get_po_date(po_name):
    """Get transaction_date from Purchase Order"""
    if not po_name:
        return None
    return frappe.db.get_value("Purchase Order", po_name, "transaction_date")


def get_pr_date(pr_name):
    """Get posting_date from Purchase Receipt"""
    if not pr_name:
        return None
    return frappe.db.get_value("Purchase Receipt", pr_name, "posting_date")


def get_delivery_status(po_qty, delivery_qty):
    """Calculate delivery status based on PO qty vs delivered qty"""
    if not po_qty:
        return "Pending"
    if delivery_qty >= po_qty:
        return "Completed"
    if delivery_qty > 0:
        return "Partial"
    return "Pending"

def build_parent_lookup(project):
    """Build a lookup dict for parent traversal: {component_master: {item_code, parent_component}}"""
    records = frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT name, item_code, parent_component
        FROM `tabProject Component Master`
        WHERE project = %s
    """, project, as_dict=True)

    return {r.name: {"item_code": r.item_code, "parent_component": r.parent_component} for r in records}


def get_ancestor_code(component_master, prefix, parent_lookup, max_depth=20):
    """
    Traverse up the parent_component chain to find an ancestor
    whose item_code starts with the given prefix (e.g., 'M' or 'G')
    """
    current = component_master
    depth = 0

    while current and depth < max_depth:
        data = parent_lookup.get(current)
        if not data:
            break

        item_code = data.get("item_code") or ""
        if item_code.startswith(prefix):
            return item_code

        current = data.get("parent_component")
        depth += 1

    return None

def build_parent_lookup(project):
    """Build a lookup dict for parent traversal: {component_master: {item_code, parent_component, m_code, g_code}}"""
    records = frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT name, item_code, parent_component, m_code, g_code
        FROM `tabProject Component Master`
        WHERE project = %s
    """, project, as_dict=True)

    return {r.name: {
        "item_code": r.item_code,
        "parent_component": r.parent_component,
        "m_code": r.m_code,
        "g_code": r.g_code
    } for r in records}


def build_bom_usage_lookup(project):
    """
    Build a lookup of bom_usage data for each Component Master.
    Returns: {cm_name: [{"m_code": ..., "g_code": ...}, ...]}

    This captures multi-parent scenarios where an item appears under multiple G-codes.
    """
    records = frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT
            cbu.parent as cm_name,
            cbu.m_code,
            cbu.g_code
        FROM `tabComponent BOM Usage` cbu
        INNER JOIN `tabProject Component Master` pcm ON pcm.name = cbu.parent
        WHERE pcm.project = %s
        AND (cbu.m_code IS NOT NULL OR cbu.g_code IS NOT NULL)
    """, project, as_dict=True)

    lookup = {}
    for r in records:
        if r.cm_name not in lookup:
            lookup[r.cm_name] = []
        lookup[r.cm_name].append({"m_code": r.m_code, "g_code": r.g_code})

    return lookup


def get_m_code_g_code(component_master, bom_usage_lookup, parent_lookup):
    """
    Get M-code and G-code(s) for a Component Master.

    Priority:
    1. bom_usage child table (for multi-parent items) - returns comma-separated G-codes
    2. Header fields (m_code, g_code on CM)
    3. Traverse parent_component chain (legacy fallback)

    Returns:
        tuple: (m_code, g_code_string)
    """
    # Check bom_usage first (handles multi-parent items)
    bom_usage_rows = bom_usage_lookup.get(component_master, [])

    if bom_usage_rows:
        m_codes = set()
        g_codes = set()

        for row in bom_usage_rows:
            if row.get("m_code"):
                m_codes.add(row["m_code"])
            if row.get("g_code"):
                g_codes.add(row["g_code"])

        # M-code should be same across all rows (one M per machine)
        m_code = list(m_codes)[0] if m_codes else None

        # G-codes may differ (multi-parent) - show as comma-separated
        g_code = ", ".join(sorted(g_codes)) if g_codes else None

        if m_code or g_code:
            return m_code, g_code

    # Fallback to header fields
    cm_data = parent_lookup.get(component_master, {})
    if cm_data.get("m_code") or cm_data.get("g_code"):
        return cm_data.get("m_code"), cm_data.get("g_code")

    # Legacy fallback: traverse parent_component chain
    m_code = get_ancestor_code(component_master, "M", parent_lookup)
    g_code = get_ancestor_code(component_master, "G", parent_lookup)

    return m_code, g_code

# Copyright (c) 2026, Bharatbodh and contributors

import frappe
from frappe import _


def execute(filters=None):
    filters = filters or {}

    if not filters.get("project"):
        return [], []

    columns = get_columns(filters)
    data = get_data(filters)

    return columns, data


def get_columns(filters):
    """Build columns based on filter toggles"""

    # Section 1: Default columns (always visible)
    columns = [
        {"label": _("Project"), "fieldname": "project", "fieldtype": "Link", "options": "Project", "width": 120},
        {"label": _("Machine Code"), "fieldname": "machine_code", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 120},
        {"label": _("WBS Code"), "fieldname": "cost_center", "fieldtype": "Link", "options": "Cost Center", "width": 150},
        {"label": _("M-Code"), "fieldname": "m_code", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 100},
        {"label": _("G-Code"), "fieldname": "g_code", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 100},
        {"label": _("Image"), "fieldname": "component_image", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 80},
        {"label": _("Item No"), "fieldname": "item_code", "fieldtype": "Link", "options": "Item", "width": 150},
        {"label": _("Item Name"), "fieldname": "item_name", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 200},
        {"label": _("Description"), "fieldname": "description", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 200},
        {"label": _("Make/Buy"), "fieldname": "make_or_buy", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 80},
        {"label": _("Qty"), "fieldname": "total_qty_limit", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 80},
        {"label": _("UOM"), "fieldname": "stock_uom", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 70},
    ]

    # Section 4: Material Request columns
    if filters.get("show_mr"):
        columns += [
            {"label": _("MR No"), "fieldname": "mr_nos", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 150},
            {"label": _("MR Qty"), "fieldname": "mr_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 80},
            {"label": _("MR ETA"), "fieldname": "mr_eta", "fieldtype": "Date", "width": 100},
            {"label": _("Pending for MR"), "fieldname": "pending_mr_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 100},
        ]

    # Section 5: RFQ & Supplier Quotation columns
    if filters.get("show_rfq"):
        # Vendor 1-4 columns (4 vendors x 4 fields = 16 columns)
        for i in range(1, 5):
            columns += [
                {"label": _(f"Vendor {i}"), "fieldname": f"vendor_{i}_name", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 120},
                {"label": _(f"V{i} Rate"), "fieldname": f"vendor_{i}_rate", "fieldtype": "Currency", "width": 90},
                {"label": _(f"V{i} Total"), "fieldname": f"vendor_{i}_total", "fieldtype": "Currency", "width": 100},
            ]

        # Comparison columns
        columns += [
            {"label": _("Lowest Vendor"), "fieldname": "lowest_vendor", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 120},
            {"label": _("Lowest Rate"), "fieldname": "lowest_rate", "fieldtype": "Currency", "width": 90},
            {"label": _("Decided Vendor"), "fieldname": "decided_vendor", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 120},
            {"label": _("Decided Rate"), "fieldname": "decided_rate", "fieldtype": "Currency", "width": 90},
        ]

    # Section 6: Purchase Order & Delivery columns
    if filters.get("show_po"):
        columns += [
            {"label": _("PO No"), "fieldname": "po_nos", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 150},
            {"label": _("PO Date"), "fieldname": "po_date", "fieldtype": "Date", "width": 100},
            {"label": _("PO Qty"), "fieldname": "po_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 80},
            {"label": _("Pending for PO"), "fieldname": "pending_po_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 100},
            {"label": _("GRN No"), "fieldname": "grn_nos", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 150},
            {"label": _("Delivery Qty"), "fieldname": "delivery_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 100},
            {"label": _("Delivery Date"), "fieldname": "delivery_date", "fieldtype": "Date", "width": 100},
            {"label": _("Pending Delivery"), "fieldname": "pending_delivery_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 110},
            {"label": _("Delivery Status"), "fieldname": "delivery_status", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 100},
        ]

    return columns


def get_data(filters):
    """Fetch all Component Masters for the project with procurement data"""

    # Get all Component Masters for the project
    component_masters = frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT
            pcm.name as component_master,
            pcm.project,
            COALESCE(pcm.cost_center, cc.name) as cost_center,
            pcm.machine_code,
            pcm.item_code,
            pcm.item_name,
            pcm.description,
            pcm.make_or_buy,
            pcm.total_qty_limit,
            pcm.component_image,
            pcm.parent_component,
            item.stock_uom
        FROM `tabProject Component Master` pcm
        LEFT JOIN `tabItem` item ON item.name = pcm.item_code
        LEFT JOIN `tabCost Center` cc ON cc.custom_machine_code = pcm.machine_code
        WHERE pcm.project = %(project)s
        ORDER BY cost_center, pcm.item_code
    """, filters, as_dict=True)

    if not component_masters:
        return []

    # Build parent lookup for M-Code and G-Code traversal (fallback)
    parent_lookup = build_parent_lookup(filters.get("project"))

    # Build bom_usage lookup for multi-parent M-Code and G-Code
    bom_usage_lookup = build_bom_usage_lookup(filters.get("project"))

    # Build procurement data maps
    procurement_data = get_procurement_data(filters.get("project"))

    data = []
    for pcm in component_masters:
        # Get M-code and G-code(s) using new structure
        m_code, g_code = get_m_code_g_code(pcm.component_master, bom_usage_lookup, parent_lookup)

        row = {
            "component_master": pcm.component_master,
            "project": pcm.project,
            "machine_code": pcm.machine_code,
            "cost_center": pcm.cost_center,
            "m_code": m_code,
            "g_code": g_code,
            "component_image": pcm.component_image,
            "item_code": pcm.item_code,
            "item_name": pcm.item_name,
            "description": pcm.description,
            "make_or_buy": pcm.make_or_buy,
            "total_qty_limit": pcm.total_qty_limit,
            "stock_uom": pcm.stock_uom,
        }

        # Add Material Request data
        if filters.get("show_mr"):
            mr_data = procurement_data.get("mr", {}).get(pcm.component_master, {})
            row.update({
                "mr_nos": mr_data.get("doc_names", ""),
                "mr_qty": mr_data.get("qty", 0),
                "mr_eta": mr_data.get("eta"),
                "pending_mr_qty": (pcm.total_qty_limit or 0) - (mr_data.get("qty") or 0),
            })

        # Add RFQ & Supplier Quotation data
        if filters.get("show_rfq"):
            sq_data = procurement_data.get("sq", {}).get(pcm.component_master, {})
            vendors = sq_data.get("vendors", [])

            # Populate vendor columns (up to 4 vendors)
            for i in range(1, 5):
                if i <= len(vendors):
                    v = vendors[i-1]
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_name"] = v.get("supplier")
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_rate"] = v.get("rate")
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_total"] = v.get("amount")
                else:
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_name"] = None
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_rate"] = None
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_total"] = None

            # Comparison columns
            row["lowest_vendor"] = sq_data.get("lowest_vendor")
            row["lowest_rate"] = sq_data.get("lowest_rate")
            row["decided_vendor"] = sq_data.get("decided_vendor")
            row["decided_rate"] = sq_data.get("decided_rate")

        # Add Purchase Order & Delivery data
        if filters.get("show_po"):
            po_data = procurement_data.get("po", {}).get(pcm.component_master, {})
            pr_data = procurement_data.get("pr", {}).get(pcm.component_master, {})

            po_qty = po_data.get("qty") or 0
            delivery_qty = pr_data.get("qty") or 0

            row.update({
                "po_nos": po_data.get("doc_names", ""),
                "po_date": po_data.get("date"),
                "po_qty": po_qty,
                "pending_po_qty": (pcm.total_qty_limit or 0) - po_qty,
                "grn_nos": pr_data.get("doc_names", ""),
                "delivery_qty": delivery_qty,
                "delivery_date": pr_data.get("date"),
                "pending_delivery_qty": po_qty - delivery_qty,
                "delivery_status": get_delivery_status(po_qty, delivery_qty),
            })

        data.append(row)

    return data


def build_parent_lookup(project):
    """Build a lookup dict for parent traversal: {component_master: {item_code, parent_component}}"""
    records = frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT name, item_code, parent_component
        FROM `tabProject Component Master`
        WHERE project = %s
    """, project, as_dict=True)

    return {r.name: {"item_code": r.item_code, "parent_component": r.parent_component} for r in records}


def get_ancestor_code(component_master, prefix, parent_lookup, max_depth=20):
    """
    Traverse up the parent_component chain to find an ancestor
    whose item_code starts with the given prefix (e.g., 'M' or 'G')
    """
    current = component_master
    depth = 0

    while current and depth < max_depth:
        data = parent_lookup.get(current)
        if not data:
            break

        item_code = data.get("item_code") or ""
        if item_code.startswith(prefix):
            return item_code

        current = data.get("parent_component")
        depth += 1

    return None


def get_procurement_data(project):
    """Fetch all procurement records and build lookup maps"""

    procurement_records = frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT
            cpr.parent as component_master,
            cpr.document_type,
            cpr.document_name,
            cpr.quantity,
            cpr.rate,
            cpr.amount
        FROM `tabComponent Procurement Record` cpr
        INNER JOIN `tabProject Component Master` pcm ON pcm.name = cpr.parent
        WHERE pcm.project = %s
    """, project, as_dict=True)

    # Initialize result structure
    result = {
        "mr": {},   # Material Request
        "sq": {},   # Supplier Quotation
        "po": {},   # Purchase Order
        "pr": {},   # Purchase Receipt
    }

    # Group records by component_master and document_type
    for rec in procurement_records:
        cm = rec.component_master
        doc_type = rec.document_type

        if doc_type == "Material Request":
            if cm not in result["mr"]:
                result["mr"][cm] = {"doc_names": [], "qty": 0, "eta": None}
            result["mr"][cm]["doc_names"].append(rec.document_name)
            result["mr"][cm]["qty"] += rec.quantity or 0

        elif doc_type == "Supplier Quotation":
            if cm not in result["sq"]:
                result["sq"][cm] = {"vendors": [], "lowest_vendor": None, "lowest_rate": None}
            result["sq"][cm]["vendors"].append({
                "supplier": get_supplier_from_sq(rec.document_name),
                "rate": rec.rate,
                "amount": rec.amount,
            })

        elif doc_type == "Purchase Order":
            if cm not in result["po"]:
                result["po"][cm] = {"doc_names": [], "qty": 0, "date": None}
            result["po"][cm]["doc_names"].append(rec.document_name)
            result["po"][cm]["qty"] += rec.quantity or 0
            # Get decided vendor info
            if cm not in result["sq"]:
                result["sq"][cm] = {"vendors": [], "lowest_vendor": None, "lowest_rate": None}
            result["sq"][cm]["decided_vendor"] = get_supplier_from_po(rec.document_name)
            result["sq"][cm]["decided_rate"] = rec.rate

        elif doc_type == "Purchase Receipt":
            if cm not in result["pr"]:
                result["pr"][cm] = {"doc_names": [], "qty": 0, "date": None}
            result["pr"][cm]["doc_names"].append(rec.document_name)
            result["pr"][cm]["qty"] += rec.quantity or 0

    # Post-process: join doc_names, find lowest vendor, get dates
    for cm, data in result["mr"].items():
        data["doc_names"] = ", ".join(list(set(data["doc_names"])))
        data["eta"] = get_mr_eta(data["doc_names"].split(", ")[0] if data["doc_names"] else None)

    for cm, data in result["sq"].items():
        # Sort vendors by rate and find lowest
        if data["vendors"]:
            data["vendors"].sort(key=lambda x: x.get("rate") or float("inf"))
            data["lowest_vendor"] = data["vendors"][0].get("supplier")
            data["lowest_rate"] = data["vendors"][0].get("rate")

    for cm, data in result["po"].items():
        data["doc_names"] = ", ".join(list(set(data["doc_names"])))
        data["date"] = get_po_date(data["doc_names"].split(", ")[0] if data["doc_names"] else None)

    for cm, data in result["pr"].items():
        data["doc_names"] = ", ".join(list(set(data["doc_names"])))
        data["date"] = get_pr_date(data["doc_names"].split(", ")[0] if data["doc_names"] else None)

    return result


def get_supplier_from_sq(sq_name):
    """Get supplier name from Supplier Quotation"""
    if not sq_name:
        return None
    return frappe.db.get_value("Supplier Quotation", sq_name, "supplier")


def get_supplier_from_po(po_name):
    """Get supplier name from Purchase Order"""
    if not po_name:
        return None
    return frappe.db.get_value("Purchase Order", po_name, "supplier")


def get_mr_eta(mr_name):
    """Get schedule_date from Material Request Item"""
    if not mr_name:
        return None
    return frappe.db.get_value("Material Request", mr_name, "schedule_date")


def get_po_date(po_name):
    """Get transaction_date from Purchase Order"""
    if not po_name:
        return None
    return frappe.db.get_value("Purchase Order", po_name, "transaction_date")


def get_pr_date(pr_name):
    """Get posting_date from Purchase Receipt"""
    if not pr_name:
        return None
    return frappe.db.get_value("Purchase Receipt", pr_name, "posting_date")


def get_delivery_status(po_qty, delivery_qty):
    """Calculate delivery status based on PO qty vs delivered qty"""
    if not po_qty:
        return "Pending"
    if delivery_qty >= po_qty:
        return "Completed"
    if delivery_qty > 0:
        return "Partial"
    return "Pending"

def get_data(filters):
    """
    Fetch all Component Masters for the project with procurement data.

    For items with multiple parents (via bom_usage), creates SEPARATE ROWS
    for each M-code/G-code combination. This shows the full procurement path:
    - M001 | G001 | A001 | qty | ...
    - M001 | G002 | A001 | qty | ...
    """

    # Get all Component Masters for the project
    component_masters = frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT
            pcm.name as component_master,
            pcm.project,
            COALESCE(pcm.cost_center, cc.name) as cost_center,
            pcm.machine_code,
            pcm.item_code,
            pcm.item_name,
            pcm.description,
            pcm.make_or_buy,
            pcm.total_qty_limit,
            pcm.component_image,
            pcm.parent_component,
            pcm.m_code as header_m_code,
            pcm.g_code as header_g_code,
            item.stock_uom
        FROM `tabProject Component Master` pcm
        LEFT JOIN `tabItem` item ON item.name = pcm.item_code
        LEFT JOIN `tabCost Center` cc ON cc.custom_machine_code = pcm.machine_code
        WHERE pcm.project = %(project)s
        ORDER BY cost_center, pcm.item_code
    """, filters, as_dict=True)

    if not component_masters:
        return []

    # Build parent lookup for M-Code and G-Code traversal (fallback)
    parent_lookup = build_parent_lookup(filters.get("project"))

    # Build bom_usage lookup: {cm_name: [{m_code, g_code, qty_per_unit}, ...]}
    bom_usage_lookup = build_bom_usage_lookup(filters.get("project"))

    # Build procurement data maps
    procurement_data = get_procurement_data(filters.get("project"))

    data = []
    for pcm in component_masters:
        # Get bom_usage rows for this CM (if any)
        bom_usage_rows = bom_usage_lookup.get(pcm.component_master, [])

        if bom_usage_rows:
            # Multi-parent: create one row per bom_usage entry
            for usage in bom_usage_rows:
                row = build_report_row(
                    pcm, usage.get("m_code"), usage.get("g_code"),
                    procurement_data, filters, parent_lookup
                )
                data.append(row)
        else:
            # Single parent or no bom_usage: use header fields or traverse
            m_code = pcm.header_m_code or get_ancestor_code(pcm.component_master, "M", parent_lookup)
            g_code = pcm.header_g_code or get_ancestor_code(pcm.component_master, "G", parent_lookup)

            row = build_report_row(
                pcm, m_code, g_code,
                procurement_data, filters, parent_lookup
            )
            data.append(row)

    return data


def build_report_row(pcm, m_code, g_code, procurement_data, filters, parent_lookup):
    """Build a single report row with the given M-code and G-code."""
    row = {
        "component_master": pcm.component_master,
        "project": pcm.project,
        "machine_code": pcm.machine_code,
        "cost_center": pcm.cost_center,
        "m_code": m_code,
        "g_code": g_code,
        "component_image": pcm.component_image,
        "item_code": pcm.item_code,
        "item_name": pcm.item_name,
        "description": pcm.description,
        "make_or_buy": pcm.make_or_buy,
        "total_qty_limit": pcm.total_qty_limit,
        "stock_uom": pcm.stock_uom,
    }

1. An item A001 can be procured as part of **multiple BOMs** (G001's BOM, G002's BOM, etc.)

3. The report needs to track **per BOM path**:
   - How much qty is required for that specific path
   - How much is procured for that path
   - How much is pending for that path

So the data model should be:
- Each bom_usage row = one procurement path
- bom_usage has: parent_bom, m_code, g_code, qty_per_unit, total_qty_required
- Procurement (MR, PO, etc.) should be tracked per G-code/BOM

|--------|--------|------|---------|--------------|---------|-----|
| M001 | G001 | A001 | 10 (from G001's BOM) | 5 (MR for G001) | 5 |
| M001 | G002 | A001 | 8 (from G002's BOM) | 8 (MR for G002) | 0 |

**Questions I need to clarify**:

content.text:
Now I understand the business requirement better!

**Scenario:**
- A001 is used in BOM-G001 (released Day 1) and BOM-G002 (released Day 30)
- When G001's BOM is released → MR created for A001 for G001's requirement
- When G002's BOM is released → Another MR created for A001 for G002's requirement
- Report needs to show procurement status **per BOM path**

**Report should show:**
| M-Code | G-Code | Item | BOM Qty | MR Qty | PO Qty | Pending |
|--------|--------|------|---------|--------|--------|---------|
| M001 | G001 | A001 | 10 | 5 | 3 | 5 |
| M001 | G002 | A001 | 8 | 8 | 8 | 0 |

**Key Question:**
When MR is created for A001, how do we track which G-code it was raised for?

Is there a field on Material Request Item like:
- `custom_g_code` or 
- `custom_parent_bom` or
- `custom_component_master` (which links to the specific usage path)?

If not, how should we link procurement to a specific G-code path? 

Options:
1. **Add a field** on MR Item to track G-code/parent_bom
2. **Aggregate at CM level** (show total procurement, not per G-code)
3. **Derive from MR creation context** (if MR is created from a specific BOM)

Please clarify how procurement is currently linked (or should be linked) to the G-code path.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - Implement M-Code and G-Code hierarchy tracking for Project Component Master system
   - Add fields to track parent_component, m_code, g_code in both bom_usage child table AND header level
   - Update Project Tracking Report to show SEPARATE ROWS for each M-code/G-code combination (multi-parent items should appear as multiple rows)
   - Track procurement (MR, PO) per BOM/G-code path since BOMs are released progressively as design is finalized
   - Key business requirement: When MR is created using "Get Items from BOM", it links to specific BOM/G-code path

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - BOM Hierarchy: Machine Code → M-Code (starts with "M") → G-Code (starts with "G") → D-Code/Raw Materials
   - Multi-parent items: Same raw material (A001) can appear under multiple G-codes (G001, G002) with different M-codes
   - NO nested G-codes under G-codes
   - bom_usage child table tracks each usage path with parent_bom, parent_component, m_code, g_code, qty_per_unit
   - Header-level m_code/g_code for items WITHOUT bom_usage (M-codes themselves, loose items)
   - Procurement is linked to BOM via MR's "Get Items from BOM" functionality with mandatory project

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **component_bom_usage.json** - Added new fields for hierarchy tracking:
     ```json
     {
       "fieldname": "parent_component",
       "fieldtype": "Link",
       "options": "Project Component Master",
       "description": "Component Master of the parent item (for hierarchy traversal)"
     },
     {
       "fieldname": "m_code",
       "fieldtype": "Data",
       "label": "M-Code",
       "read_only": 1,
       "description": "Root assembly code (item starting with M)"
     },
     {
       "fieldname": "g_code",
       "fieldtype": "Data",
       "label": "G-Code",
       "read_only": 1,
       "description": "Sub-assembly code (item starting with G)"
     }
     ```

   - **project_component_master.json** - Added m_code and g_code to header:
     ```json
     {
       "fieldname": "m_code",
       "fieldtype": "Data",
       "label": "M-Code",
       "description": "Root assembly code (item starting with M) - for items without bom_usage"
     },
     {
       "fieldname": "g_code",
       "fieldtype": "Data",
       "label": "G-Code",
       "description": "Sub-assembly code (item starting with G) - for items without bom_usage or single-parent"
     }
     ```

   - **bom_hooks.py** - Updated add_or_update_bom_usage() and added helper functions:
     ```python
     def add_or_update_bom_usage(project, item_code, parent_bom, qty_per_unit, parent_item=None):
         # Get parent Component Master for hierarchy traversal
         parent_cm = get_component_master(project, parent_item)
         parent_component = parent_cm.name if parent_cm else None
         
         # Derive M-code and G-code from parent hierarchy
         m_code, g_code = _derive_codes_from_parent(parent_cm, parent_item)
         
         # Add/update bom_usage row with all hierarchy fields
         component_master.append("bom_usage", {
             "parent_bom": parent_bom,
             "parent_component": parent_component,
             "m_code": m_code,
             "g_code": g_code,
             "qty_per_unit": qty_per_unit
         })
     
     def _derive_codes_from_parent(parent_cm, parent_item):
         # If parent starts with "M" → m_code = parent_item, g_code = NULL
         # If parent starts with "G" → m_code = parent's m_code, g_code = parent_item
         # Otherwise → traverse parent_component chain
     ```

   - **bom_upload_enhanced.py** - Added _populate_hierarchy_codes() for header-level fields:
     ```python
     def _populate_hierarchy_codes(project, only_missing=True):
         # Only processes CMs where parent_component, m_code, or g_code is NULL
         # Uses _find_parent_item_via_bom() to find parent from BOM structure
         # Derives codes based on item prefix (M, G, or other)
     ```
     Called in flow after `_link_boms_to_component_masters(project)` and before `recalculate_component_masters_for_project(project)`

   - **project_tracking.py** - Updated with bom_usage lookup (but approach was WRONG):
     - Added `build_bom_usage_lookup()` and `get_m_code_g_code()` functions
     - User rejected approach because report was combining G-codes as comma-separated instead of showing separate rows

   - **clevertech_context.md** - Added Decision 18 documentation for M-Code/G-Code hierarchy mapping

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **Report format misunderstanding**: I initially implemented the report to show comma-separated G-codes in one row. User corrected that the report should show SEPARATE ROWS for each M-code/G-code combination:
     ```
     M001 | G001 | A001 | qty | procurement
     M001 | G002 | A001 | qty | procurement
     ```
   - User rejected my edit to project_tracking.py with feedback: "please explain first what you understood and how are we planning to implement?"
   - User clarified that procurement is tracked per BOM path: "while creating MR project is mandatory and i use get items from BOM and that's how things get mapped"

5. Problem Solving:
   - Resolved hierarchy structure understanding (no nested G-codes)
   - Implemented Option B for populate_hierarchy_codes (separate function after CM creation, safer for procurement-critical CMs)
   - Still need to properly implement report to show separate rows per M-code/G-code combination with per-path procurement data

6. All User Messages:
   - "under G codes also there are sub-assemblies, so we need m-code and g-code in header too right?"

   - "Yes. so if CM present, BOM version same, then only this populate_hierarchy_codes() will run for which parent_component, m_code, g-code is null right?"
   - "yes" (to proceed with implementation)
   - "no no thats wrong did you see the existing report or not? concept is like below M001-g001-A001-project qty-procurement qty-etc etc M001-g002-A001-project qty-procurement qty-etc etc M002-g001-A001-project qty-procurement qty-etc etc M002-g003-A001-project qty-procurement qty-etc etc"
   - "please explain first what you understood and how are we planning to implement?"
   - "see A can be procured as part of multiple BOMs and we need to against which BOM how much qty is procured and how much is pending as BOM are released as and when design is finalised"
   - "while creating MR project is mandatory and i use get items from BOM and that's how things get mapped"

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Fix Project Tracking Report to show SEPARATE ROWS for each M-code/G-code combination
   - Understand how MR is linked to specific BOM/G-code via "Get Items from BOM" functionality
   - Implement per-path procurement tracking in report (qty procured per G-code, pending per G-code)

8. Current Work:

   - This links the procurement to a specific BOM/G-code path
   - Need to implement report that iterates over bom_usage rows (not CMs) to show separate rows per path with per-path procurement data

   Based on user's last message "while creating MR project is mandatory and i use get items from BOM and that's how things get mapped", the next step would be to:

    83→def get_data(filters):
    84→    """Fetch all Component Masters for the project with procurement data"""
    85→
    86→    # Get all Component Masters for the project
    87→    component_masters = frappe.db.sql("""
    88→        SELECT
    89→            pcm.name as component_master,
    90→            pcm.project,
    91→            COALESCE(pcm.cost_center, cc.name) as cost_center,
    92→            pcm.machine_code,
    93→            pcm.item_code,
    94→            pcm.item_name,
    95→            pcm.description,
    96→            pcm.make_or_buy,
    97→            pcm.total_qty_limit,
    98→            pcm.component_image,
    99→            pcm.parent_component,
   100→            item.stock_uom
   101→        FROM `tabProject Component Master` pcm
   102→        LEFT JOIN `tabItem` item ON item.name = pcm.item_code
   103→        LEFT JOIN `tabCost Center` cc ON cc.custom_machine_code = pcm.machine_code
   104→        WHERE pcm.project = %(project)s
   105→        ORDER BY cost_center, pcm.item_code
   106→    """, filters, as_dict=True)
   107→
   108→    if not component_masters:
   109→        return []
   110→
   111→    # Build parent lookup for M-Code and G-Code traversal (fallback)
   112→    parent_lookup = build_parent_lookup(filters.get("project"))

   114→    # Build bom_usage lookup for multi-parent M-Code and G-Code
   115→    bom_usage_lookup = build_bom_usage_lookup(filters.get("project"))
   116→
   117→    # Build procurement data maps
   118→    procurement_data = get_procurement_data(filters.get("project"))
   119→
   120→    data = []
   121→    for pcm in component_masters:
   122→        # Get M-code and G-code(s) using new structure
   123→        m_code, g_code = get_m_code_g_code(pcm.component_master, bom_usage_lookup, parent_lookup)
   124→
   125→        row = {
   126→            "component_master": pcm.component_master,
   127→            "project": pcm.project,
   128→            "machine_code": pcm.machine_code,
   129→            "cost_center": pcm.cost_center,
   130→            "m_code": m_code,
   131→            "g_code": g_code,
   132→            "component_image": pcm.component_image,
   133→            "item_code": pcm.item_code,
   134→            "item_name": pcm.item_name,
   135→            "description": pcm.description,
   136→            "make_or_buy": pcm.make_or_buy,
   137→            "total_qty_limit": pcm.total_qty_limit,
   138→            "stock_uom": pcm.stock_uom,
   139→        }
   140→
   141→        # Add Material Request data
   142→        if filters.get("show_mr"):
   143→            mr_data = procurement_data.get("mr", {}).get(pcm.component_master, {})
   144→            row.update({
   145→                "mr_nos": mr_data.get("doc_names", ""),
   146→                "mr_qty": mr_data.get("qty", 0),
   147→                "mr_eta": mr_data.get("eta"),
   148→                "pending_mr_qty": (pcm.total_qty_limit or 0) - (mr_data.get("qty") or 0),
   149→            })
   150→
   151→        # Add RFQ & Supplier Quotation data
   152→        if filters.get("show_rfq"):
   153→            sq_data = procurement_data.get("sq", {}).get(pcm.component_master, {})
   154→            vendors = sq_data.get("vendors", [])
   155→
   156→            # Populate vendor columns (up to 4 vendors)
   157→            for i in range(1, 5):
   158→                if i <= len(vendors):
   159→                    v = vendors[i-1]
   160→                    row[f"vendor_{i}_name"] = v.get("supplier")
   161→                    row[f"vendor_{i}_rate"] = v.get("rate")
   162→                    row[f"vendor_{i}_total"] = v.get("amount")
   163→                else:
   164→                    row[f"vendor_{i}_name"] = None
   165→                    row[f"vendor_{i}_rate"] = None
   166→                    row[f"vendor_{i}_total"] = None
   167→
   168→            # Comparison columns
   169→            row["lowest_vendor"] = sq_data.get("lowest_vendor")
   170→            row["lowest_rate"] = sq_data.get("lowest_rate")
   171→            row["decided_vendor"] = sq_data.get("decided_vendor")
   172→            row["decided_rate"] = sq_data.get("decided_rate")
   173→
   174→        # Add Purchase Order & Delivery data
   175→        if filters.get("show_po"):
   176→            po_data = procurement_data.get("po", {}).get(pcm.component_master, {})
   177→            pr_data = procurement_data.get("pr", {}).get(pcm.component_master, {})
   178→
   179→            po_qty = po_data.get("qty") or 0
   180→            delivery_qty = pr_data.get("qty") or 0
   181→
   182→            row.update({
   183→                "po_nos": po_data.get("doc_names", ""),
   184→                "po_date": po_data.get("date"),
   185→                "po_qty": po_qty,
   186→                "pending_po_qty": (pcm.total_qty_limit or 0) - po_qty,
   187→                "grn_nos": pr_data.get("doc_names", ""),
   188→                "delivery_qty": delivery_qty,
   189→                "delivery_date": pr_data.get("date"),
   190→                "pending_delivery_qty": po_qty - delivery_qty,
   191→                "delivery_status": get_delivery_status(po_qty, delivery_qty),
   192→            })
   193→
   194→        data.append(row)
   195→
   196→    return data
   197→
   198→
   199→def build_parent_lookup(project):
   200→    """Build a lookup dict for parent traversal: {component_master: {item_code, parent_component, m_code, g_code}}"""
   201→    records = frappe.db.sql("""
   202→        SELECT name, item_code, parent_component, m_code, g_code
   203→        FROM `tabProject Component Master`
   204→        WHERE project = %s
   205→    """, project, as_dict=True)
   206→
   207→    return {r.name: {
   208→        "item_code": r.item_code,
   209→        "parent_component": r.parent_component,
   210→        "m_code": r.m_code,
   211→        "g_code": r.g_code
   212→    } for r in records}
   213→
   214→
   215→def build_bom_usage_lookup(project):
   216→    """
   217→    Build a lookup of bom_usage data for each Component Master.
   218→    Returns: {cm_name: [{"m_code": ..., "g_code": ...}, ...]}
   219→
   220→    This captures multi-parent scenarios where an item appears under multiple G-codes.
   221→    """
   222→    records = frappe.db.sql("""
   223→        SELECT
   224→            cbu.parent as cm_name,
   225→            cbu.m_code,
   226→            cbu.g_code
   227→        FROM `tabComponent BOM Usage` cbu
   228→        INNER JOIN `tabProject Component Master` pcm ON pcm.name = cbu.parent
   229→        WHERE pcm.project = %s
   230→        AND (cbu.m_code IS NOT NULL OR cbu.g_code IS NOT NULL)
   231→    """, project, as_dict=True)
   232→
   233→    lookup = {}
   234→    for r in records:
   235→        if r.cm_name not in lookup:
   236→            lookup[r.cm_name] = []
   237→        lookup[r.cm_name].append({"m_code": r.m_code, "g_code": r.g_code})
   238→
   239→    return lookup
   240→
   241→
   242→def get_m_code_g_code(component_master, bom_usage_lookup, parent_lookup):
   243→    """
   244→    Get M-code and G-code(s) for a Component Master.
   245→
   246→    Priority:
   247→    1. bom_usage child table (for multi-parent items) - returns comma-separated G-codes
   248→    2. Header fields (m_code, g_code on CM)
   249→    3. Traverse parent_component chain (legacy fallback)
   250→
   251→    Returns:
   252→        tuple: (m_code, g_code_string)
   253→    """
   254→    # Check bom_usage first (handles multi-parent items)
   255→    bom_usage_rows = bom_usage_lookup.get(component_master, [])
   256→
   257→    if bom_usage_rows:
   258→        m_codes = set()
   259→        g_codes = set()
   260→
   261→        for row in bom_usage_rows:
   262→            if row.get("m_code"):
   263→                m_codes.add(row["m_code"])
   264→            if row.get("g_code"):
   265→                g_codes.add(row["g_code"])
   266→
   267→        # M-code should be same across all rows (one M per machine)
   268→        m_code = list(m_codes)[0] if m_codes else None
   269→
   270→        # G-codes may differ (multi-parent) - show as comma-separated
   271→        g_code = ", ".join(sorted(g_codes)) if g_codes else None
   272→
   273→        if m_code or g_code:
   274→            return m_code, g_code
   275→
   276→    # Fallback to header fields
   277→    cm_data = parent_lookup.get(component_master, {})
   278→    if cm_data.get("m_code") or cm_data.get("g_code"):
   279→        return cm_data.get("m_code"), cm_data.get("g_code")
   280→
   281→    # Legacy fallback: traverse parent_component chain
   282→    m_code = get_ancestor_code(component_master, "M", parent_lookup)
   283→    g_code = get_ancestor_code(component_master, "G", parent_lookup)
   284→
   285→    return m_code, g_code
   286→
   287→
   288→def get_ancestor_code(component_master, prefix, parent_lookup, max_depth=20):
   289→    """
   290→    Traverse up the parent_component chain to find an ancestor
   291→    whose item_code starts with the given prefix (e.g., 'M' or 'G')
   292→    """
   293→    current = component_master
   294→    depth = 0
   295→
   296→    while current and depth < max_depth:
   297→        data = parent_lookup.get(current)
   298→        if not data:
   299→            break
   300→
   301→        item_code = data.get("item_code") or ""
   302→        if item_code.startswith(prefix):
   303→            return item_code
   304→
   305→        current = data.get("parent_component")
   306→        depth += 1
   307→
   308→    return None
   309→
   310→
   311→def get_procurement_data(project):
   312→    """Fetch all procurement records and build lookup maps"""
   313→
   314→    procurement_records = frappe.db.sql("""
   315→        SELECT
   316→            cpr.parent as component_master,
   317→            cpr.document_type,
   318→            cpr.document_name,
   319→            cpr.quantity,
   320→            cpr.rate,
   321→            cpr.amount
   322→        FROM `tabComponent Procurement Record` cpr
   323→        INNER JOIN `tabProject Component Master` pcm ON pcm.name = cpr.parent
   324→        WHERE pcm.project = %s
   325→    """, project, as_dict=True)
   326→
   327→    # Initialize result structure
   328→    result = {
   329→        "mr": {},   # Material Request
   330→        "sq": {},   # Supplier Quotation
   331→        "po": {},   # Purchase Order
   332→        "pr": {},   # Purchase Receipt
   333→    }
   334→
   335→    # Group records by component_master and document_type
   336→    for rec in procurement_records:
   337→        cm = rec.component_master
   338→        doc_type = rec.document_type
   339→
   340→        if doc_type == "Material Request":
   341→            if cm not in result["mr"]:
   342→                result["mr"][cm] = {"doc_names": [], "qty": 0, "eta": None}
   343→            result["mr"][cm]["doc_names"].append(rec.document_name)
   344→            result["mr"][cm]["qty"] += rec.quantity or 0
   345→
   346→        elif doc_type == "Supplier Quotation":
   347→            if cm not in result["sq"]:
   348→                result["sq"][cm] = {"vendors": [], "lowest_vendor": None, "lowest_rate": None}
   349→            result["sq"][cm]["vendors"].append({
   350→                "supplier": get_supplier_from_sq(rec.document_name),
   351→                "rate": rec.rate,
   352→                "amount": rec.amount,
   353→            })
   354→
   355→        elif doc_type == "Purchase Order":
   356→            if cm not in result["po"]:
   357→                result["po"][cm] = {"doc_names": [], "qty": 0, "date": None}
   358→            result["po"][cm]["doc_names"].append(rec.document_name)

# Copyright (c) 2026, Bharatbodh and contributors
# For license information, please see license.txt

import frappe
from frappe import _


def execute(filters=None):
    filters = filters or {}

    if not filters.get("project"):
        return [], []

    columns = get_columns(filters)
    data = get_data(filters)

    return columns, data


def get_columns(filters):
    """Build columns based on filter toggles"""

    # Section 1: Default columns (always visible)
    columns = [
        {"label": _("Project"), "fieldname": "project", "fieldtype": "Link", "options": "Project", "width": 120},
        {"label": _("Machine Code"), "fieldname": "machine_code", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 120},
        {"label": _("WBS Code"), "fieldname": "cost_center", "fieldtype": "Link", "options": "Cost Center", "width": 150},
        {"label": _("M-Code"), "fieldname": "m_code", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 100},
        {"label": _("G-Code"), "fieldname": "g_code", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 100},
        {"label": _("Image"), "fieldname": "component_image", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 80},
        {"label": _("Item No"), "fieldname": "item_code", "fieldtype": "Link", "options": "Item", "width": 150},
        {"label": _("Item Name"), "fieldname": "item_name", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 200},
        {"label": _("Description"), "fieldname": "description", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 200},
        {"label": _("Make/Buy"), "fieldname": "make_or_buy", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 80},
        {"label": _("Qty"), "fieldname": "total_qty_limit", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 80},
        {"label": _("UOM"), "fieldname": "stock_uom", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 70},
    ]

    # Section 4: Material Request columns
    if filters.get("show_mr"):
        columns += [
            {"label": _("MR No"), "fieldname": "mr_nos", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 150},
            {"label": _("MR Qty"), "fieldname": "mr_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 80},
            {"label": _("MR ETA"), "fieldname": "mr_eta", "fieldtype": "Date", "width": 100},
            {"label": _("Pending for MR"), "fieldname": "pending_mr_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 100},
        ]

    # Section 5: RFQ & Supplier Quotation columns
    if filters.get("show_rfq"):
        # Vendor 1-4 columns (4 vendors x 4 fields = 16 columns)
        for i in range(1, 5):
            columns += [
                {"label": _(f"Vendor {i}"), "fieldname": f"vendor_{i}_name", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 120},
                {"label": _(f"V{i} Rate"), "fieldname": f"vendor_{i}_rate", "fieldtype": "Currency", "width": 90},
                {"label": _(f"V{i} Total"), "fieldname": f"vendor_{i}_total", "fieldtype": "Currency", "width": 100},
            ]

        # Comparison columns
        columns += [
            {"label": _("Lowest Vendor"), "fieldname": "lowest_vendor", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 120},
            {"label": _("Lowest Rate"), "fieldname": "lowest_rate", "fieldtype": "Currency", "width": 90},
            {"label": _("Decided Vendor"), "fieldname": "decided_vendor", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 120},
            {"label": _("Decided Rate"), "fieldname": "decided_rate", "fieldtype": "Currency", "width": 90},
        ]

    # Section 6: Purchase Order & Delivery columns
    if filters.get("show_po"):
        columns += [
            {"label": _("PO No"), "fieldname": "po_nos", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 150},
            {"label": _("PO Date"), "fieldname": "po_date", "fieldtype": "Date", "width": 100},
            {"label": _("PO Qty"), "fieldname": "po_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 80},
            {"label": _("Pending for PO"), "fieldname": "pending_po_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 100},
            {"label": _("GRN No"), "fieldname": "grn_nos", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 150},
            {"label": _("Delivery Qty"), "fieldname": "delivery_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 100},
            {"label": _("Delivery Date"), "fieldname": "delivery_date", "fieldtype": "Date", "width": 100},
            {"label": _("Pending Delivery"), "fieldname": "pending_delivery_qty", "fieldtype": "Float", "width": 110},
            {"label": _("Delivery Status"), "fieldname": "delivery_status", "fieldtype": "Data", "width": 100},
        ]

    return columns


def get_data(filters):
    """Fetch all Component Masters for the project with procurement data"""

    # Get all Component Masters for the project
    component_masters = frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT
            pcm.name as component_master,
            pcm.project,
            COALESCE(pcm.cost_center, cc.name) as cost_center,
            pcm.machine_code,
            pcm.item_code,
            pcm.item_name,
            pcm.description,
            pcm.make_or_buy,
            pcm.total_qty_limit,
            pcm.component_image,
            pcm.parent_component,
            item.stock_uom
        FROM `tabProject Component Master` pcm
        LEFT JOIN `tabItem` item ON item.name = pcm.item_code
        LEFT JOIN `tabCost Center` cc ON cc.custom_machine_code = pcm.machine_code
        WHERE pcm.project = %(project)s
        ORDER BY cost_center, pcm.item_code
    """, filters, as_dict=True)

    if not component_masters:
        return []

    # Build parent lookup for M-Code and G-Code traversal (fallback)
    parent_lookup = build_parent_lookup(filters.get("project"))

    # Build bom_usage lookup for multi-parent M-Code and G-Code
    bom_usage_lookup = build_bom_usage_lookup(filters.get("project"))

    # Build procurement data maps
    procurement_data = get_procurement_data(filters.get("project"))

    data = []
    for pcm in component_masters:
        # Get M-code and G-code(s) using new structure
        m_code, g_code = get_m_code_g_code(pcm.component_master, bom_usage_lookup, parent_lookup)

        row = {
            "component_master": pcm.component_master,
            "project": pcm.project,
            "machine_code": pcm.machine_code,
            "cost_center": pcm.cost_center,
            "m_code": m_code,
            "g_code": g_code,
            "component_image": pcm.component_image,
            "item_code": pcm.item_code,
            "item_name": pcm.item_name,
            "description": pcm.description,
            "make_or_buy": pcm.make_or_buy,
            "total_qty_limit": pcm.total_qty_limit,
            "stock_uom": pcm.stock_uom,
        }

        # Add Material Request data
        if filters.get("show_mr"):
            mr_data = procurement_data.get("mr", {}).get(pcm.component_master, {})
            row.update({
                "mr_nos": mr_data.get("doc_names", ""),
                "mr_qty": mr_data.get("qty", 0),
                "mr_eta": mr_data.get("eta"),
                "pending_mr_qty": (pcm.total_qty_limit or 0) - (mr_data.get("qty") or 0),
            })

        # Add RFQ & Supplier Quotation data
        if filters.get("show_rfq"):
            sq_data = procurement_data.get("sq", {}).get(pcm.component_master, {})
            vendors = sq_data.get("vendors", [])

            # Populate vendor columns (up to 4 vendors)
            for i in range(1, 5):
                if i <= len(vendors):
                    v = vendors[i-1]
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_name"] = v.get("supplier")
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_rate"] = v.get("rate")
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_total"] = v.get("amount")
                else:
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_name"] = None
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_rate"] = None
                    row[f"vendor_{i}_total"] = None

            # Comparison columns
            row["lowest_vendor"] = sq_data.get("lowest_vendor")
            row["lowest_rate"] = sq_data.get("lowest_rate")
            row["decided_vendor"] = sq_data.get("decided_vendor")
            row["decided_rate"] = sq_data.get("decided_rate")

        # Add Purchase Order & Delivery data
        if filters.get("show_po"):
            po_data = procurement_data.get("po", {}).get(pcm.component_master, {})
            pr_data = procurement_data.get("pr", {}).get(pcm.component_master, {})

            po_qty = po_data.get("qty") or 0
            delivery_qty = pr_data.get("qty") or 0

            row.update({
                "po_nos": po_data.get("doc_names", ""),
                "po_date": po_data.get("date"),
                "po_qty": po_qty,
                "pending_po_qty": (pcm.total_qty_limit or 0) - po_qty,
                "grn_nos": pr_data.get("doc_names", ""),
                "delivery_qty": delivery_qty,
                "delivery_date": pr_data.get("date"),
                "pending_delivery_qty": po_qty - delivery_qty,
                "delivery_status": get_delivery_status(po_qty, delivery_qty),
            })

        data.append(row)

    return data


def build_parent_lookup(project):
    """Build a lookup dict for parent traversal: {component_master: {item_code, parent_component, m_code, g_code}}"""
    records = frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT name, item_code, parent_component, m_code, g_code
        FROM `tabProject Component Master`
        WHERE project = %s
    """, project, as_dict=True)

    return {r.name: {
        "item_code": r.item_code,
        "parent_component": r.parent_component,
        "m_code": r.m_code,
        "g_code": r.g_code
    } for r in records}


def build_bom_usage_lookup(project):
    """
    Build a lookup of bom_usage data for each Component Master.
    Returns: {cm_name: [{"m_code": ..., "g_code": ...}, ...]}

    This captures multi-parent scenarios where an item appears under multiple G-codes.
    """
    records = frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT
            cbu.parent as cm_name,
            cbu.m_code,
            cbu.g_code
        FROM `tabComponent BOM Usage` cbu
        INNER JOIN `tabProject Component Master` pcm ON pcm.name = cbu.parent
        WHERE pcm.project = %s
        AND (cbu.m_code IS NOT NULL OR cbu.g_code IS NOT NULL)
    """, project, as_dict=True)

    lookup = {}
    for r in records:
        if r.cm_name not in lookup:
            lookup[r.cm_name] = []
        lookup[r.cm_name].append({"m_code": r.m_code, "g_code": r.g_code})

    return lookup


def get_m_code_g_code(component_master, bom_usage_lookup, parent_lookup):
    """
    Get M-code and G-code(s) for a Component Master.

    Priority:
    1. bom_usage child table (for multi-parent items) - returns comma-separated G-codes
    2. Header fields (m_code, g_code on CM)
    3. Traverse parent_component chain (legacy fallback)

    Returns:
        tuple: (m_code, g_code_string)
    """
    # Check bom_usage first (handles multi-parent items)
    bom_usage_rows = bom_usage_lookup.get(component_master, [])

    if bom_usage_rows:
        m_codes = set()
        g_codes = set()

        for row in bom_usage_rows:
            if row.get("m_code"):
                m_codes.add(row["m_code"])
            if row.get("g_code"):
                g_codes.add(row["g_code"])

        # M-code should be same across all rows (one M per machine)
        m_code = list(m_codes)[0] if m_codes else None

        # G-codes may differ (multi-parent) - show as comma-separated
        g_code = ", ".join(sorted(g_codes)) if g_codes else None

        if m_code or g_code:
            return m_code, g_code

    # Fallback to header fields
    cm_data = parent_lookup.get(component_master, {})
    if cm_data.get("m_code") or cm_data.get("g_code"):
        return cm_data.get("m_code"), cm_data.get("g_code")

    # Legacy fallback: traverse parent_component chain
    m_code = get_ancestor_code(component_master, "M", parent_lookup)
    g_code = get_ancestor_code(component_master, "G", parent_lookup)

    return m_code, g_code


def get_ancestor_code(component_master, prefix, parent_lookup, max_depth=20):
    """
    Traverse up the parent_component chain to find an ancestor
    whose item_code starts with the given prefix (e.g., 'M' or 'G')
    """
    current = component_master
    depth = 0

    while current and depth < max_depth:
        data = parent_lookup.get(current)
        if not data:
            break

        item_code = data.get("item_code") or ""
        if item_code.startswith(prefix):
            return item_code

        current = data.get("parent_component")
        depth += 1

    return None


def get_procurement_data(project):
    """Fetch all procurement records and build lookup maps"""

    procurement_records = frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT
            cpr.parent as component_master,
            cpr.document_type,
            cpr.document_name,
            cpr.quantity,
            cpr.rate,
            cpr.amount
        FROM `tabComponent Procurement Record` cpr
        INNER JOIN `tabProject Component Master` pcm ON pcm.name = cpr.parent
        WHERE pcm.project = %s
    """, project, as_dict=True)

    # Initialize result structure
    result = {
        "mr": {},   # Material Request
        "sq": {},   # Supplier Quotation
        "po": {},   # Purchase Order
        "pr": {},   # Purchase Receipt
    }

    # Group records by component_master and document_type
    for rec in procurement_records:
        cm = rec.component_master
        doc_type = rec.document_type

        if doc_type == "Material Request":
            if cm not in result["mr"]:
                result["mr"][cm] = {"doc_names": [], "qty": 0, "eta": None}
            result["mr"][cm]["doc_names"].append(rec.document_name)
            result["mr"][cm]["qty"] += rec.quantity or 0

        elif doc_type == "Supplier Quotation":
            if cm not in result["sq"]:
                result["sq"][cm] = {"vendors": [], "lowest_vendor": None, "lowest_rate": None}
            result["sq"][cm]["vendors"].append({
                "supplier": get_supplier_from_sq(rec.document_name),
                "rate": rec.rate,
                "amount": rec.amount,
            })

        elif doc_type == "Purchase Order":
            if cm not in result["po"]:
                result["po"][cm] = {"doc_names": [], "qty": 0, "date": None}
            result["po"][cm]["doc_names"].append(rec.document_name)
            result["po"][cm]["qty"] += rec.quantity or 0
            # Get decided vendor info
            if cm not in result["sq"]:
                result["sq"][cm] = {"vendors": [], "lowest_vendor": None, "lowest_rate": None}
            result["sq"][cm]["decided_vendor"] = get_supplier_from_po(rec.document_name)
            result["sq"][cm]["decided_rate"] = rec.rate

        elif doc_type == "Purchase Receipt":
            if cm not in result["pr"]:
                result["pr"][cm] = {"doc_names": [], "qty": 0, "date": None}
            result["pr"][cm]["doc_names"].append(rec.document_name)
            result["pr"][cm]["qty"] += rec.quantity or 0

    # Post-process: join doc_names, find lowest vendor, get dates
    for cm, data in result["mr"].items():
        data["doc_names"] = ", ".join(list(set(data["doc_names"])))
        data["eta"] = get_mr_eta(data["doc_names"].split(", ")[0] if data["doc_names"] else None)

    for cm, data in result["sq"].items():
        # Sort vendors by rate and find lowest
        if data["vendors"]:
            data["vendors"].sort(key=lambda x: x.get("rate") or float("inf"))
            data["lowest_vendor"] = data["vendors"][0].get("supplier")
            data["lowest_rate"] = data["vendors"][0].get("rate")

    for cm, data in result["po"].items():
        data["doc_names"] = ", ".join(list(set(data["doc_names"])))
        data["date"] = get_po_date(data["doc_names"].split(", ")[0] if data["doc_names"] else None)

    for cm, data in result["pr"].items():
        data["doc_names"] = ", ".join(list(set(data["doc_names"])))
        data["date"] = get_pr_date(data["doc_names"].split(", ")[0] if data["doc_names"] else None)

    return result


def get_supplier_from_sq(sq_name):
    """Get supplier name from Supplier Quotation"""
    if not sq_name:
        return None
    return frappe.db.get_value("Supplier Quotation", sq_name, "supplier")


def get_supplier_from_po(po_name):
    """Get supplier name from Purchase Order"""
    if not po_name:
        return None
    return frappe.db.get_value("Purchase Order", po_name, "supplier")


def get_mr_eta(mr_name):
    """Get schedule_date from Material Request Item"""
    if not mr_name:
        return None
    return frappe.db.get_value("Material Request", mr_name, "schedule_date")


def get_po_date(po_name):
    """Get transaction_date from Purchase Order"""
    if not po_name:
        return None
    return frappe.db.get_value("Purchase Order", po_name, "transaction_date")


def get_pr_date(pr_name):
    """Get posting_date from Purchase Receipt"""
    if not pr_name:
        return None
    return frappe.db.get_value("Purchase Receipt", pr_name, "posting_date")


def get_delivery_status(po_qty, delivery_qty):
    """Calculate delivery status based on PO qty vs delivered qty"""
    if not po_qty:
        return "Pending"
    if delivery_qty >= po_qty:
        return "Completed"
    if delivery_qty > 0:
        return "Partial"
    return "Pending"

content.thinking:
Now I understand the current report structure. The problem is:

1. Current approach: Iterates over Component Masters (one row per item)
2. Required approach: Iterate over bom_usage rows (one row per M-code/G-code combination)

The key changes needed:

1. **Data Structure Change**: Instead of one row per Component Master, we need one row per `bom_usage` entry.
   - For raw material A001 with 2 bom_usage rows (G001, G002), we show 2 rows in report

2. **Procurement Tracking**: Track MR/PO per bom_no path
   - MR Item has `bom_no` field
   - bom_usage has `parent_bom` field
   - Match: MR Item.bom_no = bom_usage.parent_bom

Let me plan the implementation:

## New get_data() logic:

```python
def get_data(filters):
    # 1. Get all bom_usage rows for the project with CM info
    bom_usage_rows = frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT
            cbu.parent as component_master,
            cbu.parent_bom,
            cbu.m_code,
            cbu.g_code,
            cbu.qty_per_unit,
            pcm.project,
            pcm.machine_code,
            pcm.item_code,
            pcm.item_name,
            pcm.description,
            pcm.make_or_buy,
            pcm.total_qty_limit,
            pcm.component_image,
            pcm.cost_center,
            item.stock_uom
        FROM `tabComponent BOM Usage` cbu
        INNER JOIN `tabProject Component Master` pcm ON pcm.name = cbu.parent
        LEFT JOIN `tabItem` item ON item.name = pcm.item_code
        LEFT JOIN `tabCost Center` cc ON cc.custom_machine_code = pcm.machine_code
        WHERE pcm.project = %(project)s
        ORDER BY cbu.m_code, cbu.g_code, pcm.item_code
    """, filters, as_dict=True)
    
    # 2. Also get CMs WITHOUT bom_usage (M-codes themselves, loose items)
    # These use header-level m_code, g_code
    cms_without_bom_usage = frappe.db.sql("""
        SELECT
            pcm.name as component_master,
            NULL as parent_bom,
            pcm.m_code,
            pcm.g_code,
            1 as qty_per_unit,
            pcm.project,
            pcm.machine_code,
            pcm.item_code,
            pcm.item_name,
            pcm.description,
            pcm.make_or_buy,
            pcm.total_qty_limit,
            pcm.component_image,
            pcm.cost_center,
            item.stock_uom
        FROM `tabProject Component Master` pcm
        LEFT JOIN `tabItem` item ON item.name = pcm.item_code
        WHERE pcm.project = %(project)s
        AND NOT EXISTS (
            SELECT 1 FROM `tabComponent BOM Usage` cbu WHERE cbu.parent = pcm.name
        )
        ORDER BY pcm.m_code, pcm.g_code, pcm.item_code
    """, filters, as_dict=True)
```

This approach handles both bom_usage entries and standalone component masters, combining them into a single dataset where each row represents either a BOM usage relationship or a direct M-code/G-code assignment. For each row in the combined dataset, I'm looking up the corresponding procurement data using a key of item_code and parent_bom. The procurement_data dictionary is organized by material request and purchase order information, keyed by this same (item_code, bom_no) pair to track procurement activity for each specific BOM path.
